title: De imperatief · Commands
subtitle: Drop -en. Soften with even / eens / maar.

questions.explanation: Basic rule: drop -en from the infinitive. komen → Kom! Same form as the ik-stem.
Long-vowel rule: the single 'e' in 'stu-de-ren' is in an open syllable (long). When you drop -en to close the syllable, you double to 'ee' to keep the long sound. studeren → Studeer!

questions.q: Which is the correct imperative of the separable verb 'opstaan'?
questions.explanation: Separable verbs split. The main verb form is 'sta' (drop -en) and the prefix 'op' goes to the end: Sta op! Same pattern: uitgaan → Ga uit!, aandoen → Doe aan!

questions.q: How do you soften an imperative in Belgian Dutch?
questions.explanation: Bare imperatives sound abrupt in Belgian Dutch. Adding 'even', 'eens', 'maar' or 'alstublieft' makes them polite without changing the verb. 'Kom even hier.' is much friendlier than 'Kom hier!'

questions.q: Which form is the FORMAL imperative (with 'u')?
questions.explanation: Formal imperative with 'u' uses stem + t: 'Komt u binnen.' This is used for very formal contexts (official forms, polite written requests). For everyday Belgian Dutch, the bare-stem form with 'alstublieft' is more natural.

questions.q: 'Vergeet je paraplu niet!' — what makes this an imperative?
questions.explanation: 'Vergeet' is the imperative form of 'vergeten' (basic rule: drop -en). The negation 'niet' goes near the end. Whole sentence: 'Don't forget your umbrella!'
